reviews.review_msg: Struggled at first but Dr. Warriner gave me some great advice on how to write a philosophy paper and I nailed the remaining papers. Her lectures can be tricky to follow but that was the nature of the class, but she was very conscious of backtracking and making sure her point was presented clearly. Would definitely take her class again.

Jennifer was an amazing professor! She really made sure to establish a safe learning environment and include everyone's opinions, as well as make herself available outside of class hours to help explain concepts and paper topics. Her grading is very fair and her feedback is insanely detailed. Lectures also aren't mandatory but are super helpful!

I took PHIL 321 and 326 with Dr. Warriner and I enjoyed both classes. Specifically, it's nice to have a phil prof with real world experiences, and on top of that she was also an excellent teacher and fair marker. I would definitely take a class with her again.

Took bioethics with her and loved it!! Very good at carrying class discussions and is accessible for feedback and help. Slightly confused by her grading as I did really well on some essays and others not so much even though I applied her feedback before submitting them all. Very passionate and knowledgable and I looked forward to class every week!
She is amazing. Made me want to do a minor in philosophy. Course is heavily curved. If you want to do well make sure you really understand the concepts, go to all tutorials and ask questions!

The MC on the midterm and finals were very tricky but the short answer's were easy. The TA's gave low marks to people on the papers, and never really taught us how to write a good philosophy paper. They always kept telling us that a philosophy paper is different from all the papers you've ever wrote. So, shouldn't they teach you it first?

Jen is a good prof. This was not my first Phil course,I aced 105 and 100W but I have no idea how people (if any) got an A in this class. The average was 72 and was curved to become a B- for everyone. The TA's marking seemed to clump everyone together. I had no idea how to ace assignments or tests and "the right way" was never explained. Frustrating

No group work, records lectures, tries to be as thorough as possible in the 50 minutes given. Lectures are well thought out, her teaching ability is amazing if you pay attention. Gives the general gist of what the philosophers are talking about, but honestly your grade will mostly depend on which TA you get. Get Anthony if you can.

Records lectures which is nice. Goes over everything you need to know about the readings and only tests from what she talks about in lecture, but being familiar with the readings is a must. Get Anthony as your TA, he's the nicest guy and incredibly helpful, and I would not have done as well without him.
She only has 30 minute office hours and that shows how little care she has for her students. Her midterms are extremely impractical and they test you on the most pointless topics.
